LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE CONTINUED FROM NOV-04-2002 TO LAW AND MOTION AT NOV-18-2002, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 PER EX PARTE APPLICATION OF OCT. 28, 2002.
MINI-MINUTES FOR NOV-18-2002 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. A R G U E D AND THE COURT DENIED THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. THE COURT DIRECTED RESPONDENT BLUE SHIELD TO PREPARE A DETAILED STATEMENT OF REASONS. COUNSEL SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS AFTER RECEIPT OF THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT TO PREPARE THIS STATEMENT. THEREAFTER, OPPOSING COUNSEL SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS IN WHICH TO REVIEW THIS STATEMENT AND TO MAKE WHATEVER OBJECTIONS HE DEEMS NECESSARY. (AJR/302/PB) KAREN FARNSWORTH, CSR 5162

calendar.matters:
Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Nov-26-2002 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel K. Denenberg, Esq. 925/943-6650 for Petitioner
Lawrence J. Rose, Esq. of EPSTEIN BECKER & GREEN, P.C. 398-3500 and Charles L. Sweeris, Esq. 229-5107 for Respondent BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA
Greg Abrams, Esq. of CENTER FOR LEGAL AFFAIRS 882-3350 appearing as amicus curiae CALIFORNIA MEDICAL ASSOCIATION
* A R G U E D and the Court denied the petition for writ of mandate. The Court directed respondent BLUE SHIELD to prepare a detailed statement of reasons. Counsel shall have 10 days after receipt of the Reporter's Transcript to prepare this statement. Thereafter, opposing counsel shall have 10 days in which to review this Statement and to make whatever objections he deems necessary. (AJR/302/PB) Karen Farnsworth, CSR 5162
